NetSuite ↔ MonetizeNow Integration Reference

Stream 2 of 3 Workato flows · Highest probability of full native migration

Architecture overview
Detailed breakdown
What we need from Mercell
Full RevOps architecture all streams
This document covers the MonetizeNow ↔ NetSuite stream (right pipe). HubSpot stream covered in separate doc.
Data flow: MonetizeNow ↔ NetSuite

Everything between MonetizeNow and NetSuite routes through Workato. This stream represents ~30-35% of Mercell's total Workato spend.

Why Workato exists here

Mercell onboarded with MN before MN had a native NetSuite integration. Workato built the entire NS architecture from scratch. Since then, MN has shipped a full native NS integration but nobody has revisited Mercell's setup. Every NS improvement MN has shipped in the last 2 years is invisible to Mercell.

service credits, customers

middleware

invoices, credit memos,
payments, items

financial ledger

Manual SC sync tickets (9mo)

Immediate win: service credit sync

39 manual "sync service credit to NS" tickets in 9 months. Same task every time, no investigation needed. MN native NS integration handles credit memos automatically. Enabling this one flow eliminates ~20% of all Mercell support volume overnight.

Recommended migration approach
PhaseTimelineWhat happensRisk
1. ValidateBefore Aug 1Get Workato recipe inventory, map to MN native capabilities, demo to MercellNone
2. Parallel runAugustEnable MN native NS in staging, run alongside Workato in production, compare outputLow
3. SC cutover firstSepDisable Workato for service credits only (lowest risk, highest ROI)Very low
4. Full cutoverOctDisable remaining Workato NS recipes one by one, monitor 2 weeks eachMedium
A. Invoice sync CORE
Direction: MN → NS (invoices generated in MN push to NS)
Invoice creation and sync
MN generates invoices from billing schedules, pushes to NS as the financial ledger
MN native available

What Workato does today

  • Reads invoices from MN (via API or webhook)
  • Maps MN invoice fields to NS invoice record fields
  • Maps MN products to NS items for line items
  • Routes to correct NS subsidiary based on legal entity
  • Handles multi-currency conversion
  • Sometimes fails requires manual re-push (15+ tickets)

What MN native does

  • Creates NS invoices automatically when generated in MN
  • Maps MN products to NS items (configurable)
  • Handles subsidiary routing via legal entity mapping
  • Supports multi-currency invoicing
  • Pushes line items with amounts, quantities, descriptions
  • Retries on failure with error logging

Known issues with current Workato setup

  • $0 invoices (free trial periods) rejected by NS #4567
  • Invoice sync failures requiring manual intervention #4691, #4734, #5123
  • Unusual NS invoice sync activity #4344
  • Discarded NS IDs on re-sync #3801
  • PO field mismatch between quote and invoice #5256

What we need from Xuetao

  • NS item mapping: how does Workato map MN products to NS items? 1:1 or aggregated?
  • How many NS subsidiaries? What routing rules?
  • Does Workato do tax calculations or rounding adjustments?
  • How does Workato handle $0 invoices?

MN native solves the $0 invoice problem

MN's Free Trials feature consolidates free-period billing onto the first paid invoice no $0 invoice is generated, so NS never rejects it.

Evidence: #4691, #4734, #5123 (invoice sync requests), #4344 (unusual sync), #3801 (discarded NS IDs), #4567 (free months), #5256 (PO mismatch), #4831 (offering ID in NS), #4995 (invoice settings by country)
B. Service credit sync HIGHEST ROI
Direction: MN → NS
Service credits to NetSuite
39 manual tickets in 9 months the single biggest operational waste
Immediate win

What Workato does today (or doesn't)

  • Service credits are NOT auto-syncing via Workato
  • Every SC requires Eli to create a Pylon ticket → MN manually triggers sync → confirms
  • 39 identical tickets in 9 months
  • ~10 hours of both teams' time wasted
  • Either Workato was never configured for SC sync, or it's broken/disabled

What MN native does

  • MN native NS integration pushes credit memos to NS automatically
  • No manual intervention needed
  • Credit notes created in MN auto-sync when native integration is enabled
  • This alone eliminates 20% of all Mercell support volume

One question for Xuetao

Why is service credit sync manual? Was a Workato recipe never built for this, or is one broken? The answer determines whether we just enable MN native (if no recipe exists) or need to disable a broken recipe first.

Tickets: #1944, #2161, #2211, #2222, #2244, #2258, #2321, #2373, #2390, #2482, #2497, #2532, #2635, #2654, #2746, #2749, #2929, #3094, #3263, #3405, #3413, #3429, #3683, #3705, #3931, #4068, #4267, #4414, #4472, #4534, #4535, #4543, #4544, #4552, #4610, #4806, #4885, #5027, #5028
C. Credit note sync CORE
Direction: MN → NS
Credit notes from amendments
When a contract amendment generates a credit, it needs to reach NS
MN native available

What Workato does

  • Pushes credit notes from MN to NS credit memos
  • Maps credit reason codes
  • Amounts vs applied amounts sometimes mismatch (#4373)
  • Custom credit reason fields configured via Workato (#3515)

What MN native does

  • Credit memos auto-sync to NS when native integration is enabled
  • Supports reason codes
  • Auto-creates when amendment generates a credit in MN

What we need from Xuetao

  • How does Workato map credit reason codes to NS?
  • Are there NS workflows triggered by incoming credit memos (e.g., auto-apply to open invoices)?
  • Applied amount vs total amount: does Workato do any splitting logic?
Evidence: #4373 (credit note amounts mismatch), #3515 (custom credit reasons), #4973, #4901 (credit reason errors), #3411 (credit note avoidance on amendment), #5009 (credit note creation)
D. Payment sync UNKNOWN
Direction: Unclear likely NS → MN or external gateway → both
Payment recording and reconciliation
How do payments flow between MN and NS?
Direction unknown

What Workato likely does

  • Records payment status from NS back to MN to mark invoices paid
  • Or: records payment from a gateway and pushes to both MN and NS
  • Limited direct evidence no payment-specific tickets found

Critical unknowns

  • Where do payments originate? NS direct? Payment gateway? Manual?
  • Does MN need to know about payment status, or is NS the only consumer?
  • Is Mercell using any payment gateway connected to MN (Stripe, Adyen)?
  • PSP initiative (from QBR) will change this flow need to plan ahead
E. Customer / account sync SUPPORTING
Direction: MN → NS (customer record creation)
Customer records in NetSuite
MN native available

MN creates/links NS customer records when contracts are processed. Native integration handles subsidiary mapping based on MN account fields. Workato likely handles the same need to confirm if there's any additional logic (custom fields, special routing).

Evidence: #4415 (NS sync for user data), #3963 (legal entity), #4304 (ISO country codes)
F. Product / item mapping SUPPORTING
Direction: MN → NS (product-to-item mapping for invoice lines)
How MN products become NS items
MN native available

MN native integration creates NS items for each product and maps them in the connector settings. Need to confirm: does Workato do 1:1 mapping or aggregate line items? Are there NS revenue recognition rules tied to specific items that would break if we change the mapping?

Evidence: #4831 (MN offering ID in NS question about how IDs map)
Information needed from Mercell

Prioritized list for Xuetao. The NS stream is the most likely candidate for full native migration these items determine the timeline.

The case for native migration

Cost of staying on Workato

  • ~30-35% of total Workato spend on this stream
  • People cost for maintenance (Xuetao's team)
  • Every MN billing improvement is invisible to NS
  • Fragility: "10 things I want to roll out but can't" (Rak)
  • Built in 2023-24 by people who may no longer maintain it
  • Blocks credit-based billing pivot

Risk of migrating

  • "Working like a well-oiled machine" disruption risk
  • Unknown custom transformations in recipes
  • NS rev rec rules may be tied to current item structure
  • Mitigation: parallel run + phased cutover starting with SC (lowest risk)
